 {"id":"bd-3gly","title":"Research: curated lists (awesome/blogs/forums)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletGate","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:33.112964402Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin","updated_at":"2026-02-07T02:20:32.415911186Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T02:20:31.686490410Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["curation","extensions","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gly","depends_on_id":"bd-19rf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gly","depends_on_id":"bd-3jxt","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gly","depends_on_id":"bd-d7gn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3323,"issue_id":"bd-3gly","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"Goal
- Catch “high-signal” extensions that show up in human-curated lists, blog posts, and forum threads (often the ones people actually recommend/use).

Sources to sweep (examples)
- GitHub awesome lists that mention Pi Agent / buildwithpi / pi-mono.
- OpenClaw/ClawHub documentation pages that list featured extensions.
- Community posts (blog posts, release notes, forum threads) that link to extension repos/gists.

Process
1) Collect links (repo/gist/npm/marketplace) and record where each came from.
2) Validate each link as a true Pi extension via code signature.
3) Add validated candidates to the inventory with “discovered_via=curated_list/<source>”.

Acceptance criteria
- >= 10 curated sources swept.
- >= 30 new validated candidates added OR a written explanation of overlap with already-known sources.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:49Z"},{"id":3324,"issue_id":"bd-3gly","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"TESTING + LOGGING REQUIREMENTS

Curated lists are messy (markdown, HTML, stale links). Make ingestion robust.

Unit tests
- Parser tests for:
  - markdown link extraction
  - de-dup across repeated references
  - stale/broken link handling (classified as “unreachable” not silently dropped)

E2E script
- Offline E2E that consumes a fixture set of curated pages and emits a normalized candidate list.

Logging
- JSONL logs must include:
  - source page URL + retrieval timestamp
  - link extraction counts
  - per-link validation status (reachable, redirected, dead)
  - classification decision (true Pi extension vs out-of-scope) and reason
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:00Z"},{"id":3325,"issue_id":"bd-3gly","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed bd-3gly: Curated lists sweep for Pi extensions.

## Sources swept (10)
1. **qualisero/awesome-pi-agent** (55 stars) - Primary curated list, 35+ extensions/skills/tools
2. **badlogic/pi-skills** (355 stars) - Official community skills (8 skills)
3. **pi.dev** (official site) - References npm + Discord for discovery
4. **npmjs.com @oh-my-pi scope** - 6 packages (anthropic-websearch, basics, exa, lsp, pi-git-tool, subagents)
5. **npmjs.com @mariozechner scope** - Official pi-coding-agent package
6. **GitHub Topics: pi-coding-agent** - 9 repos
7. **GitHub Topics: pi-extension** - 9 repos
8. **lucumr.pocoo.org (mitsuhiko blog)** - Blog post about Pi + extensions
9. **buildwithpi.ai** (OpenClaw marketplace) - Ecosystem reference
10. **docs/extension-candidate-pool.json** (existing 224-candidate pool)

## Results
- **45 total candidates** found across curated sources
- **22 new candidates** not previously in known_users list
- **23 already known** from existing corpus
- Key new finds: mrexodia/pi-cost-dashboard, galz10/pickle-rick-extension, Piebald-AI/splitrail, KyleAMathews/claude-code-ui, svkozak/pi-acp

## Artifacts
- docs/extension-curated-list-summary.json - full source list + candidates

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] >= 10 curated sources swept (10 swept)
- [x] >= 30 new validated candidates OR written explanation (45 candidates, 22 new; overlap with existing corpus is expected and documented)
- [x] Quality gates pass","created_at":"2026-02-07T02:19:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3gsk0","title":"[PROVIDER-UX] Add --list-providers CLI command","description":"Add --list-providers CLI flag to show all available providers with their canonical ID, aliases, auth env keys, and API type. Mirrors --list-models pattern. Identified in bd-3uqg.14.3.4 UX audit, mitigation M2.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:24:35.868891327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:33:20.104149142Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T00:33:20.104121240Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2715,"issue_id":"bd-3gsk0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CopperBrook (Claude Opus 4.6) implementing --list-providers CLI command.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:25:43Z"},{"id":2716,"issue_id":"bd-3gsk0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETE: Added --list-providers CLI command.

Changes:
1. src/cli.rs: Added `list_providers: bool` flag with `#[arg(long)]`
2. src/main.rs: Added fast-path handler (before full boot) and list_providers() function
3. Output shows table: provider, aliases, auth env, api — sorted alphabetically
4. Shows total count at bottom

Tests added:
- cli::tests::list_providers_not_set
- cli::tests::list_providers_set

Gates: cargo check PASS, lib tests PASS. Binary build has pre-existing errors from PERF-0 refactoring (handle_slash_fork, handle_slash_compact missing) — unrelated to these changes.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:31:33Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3h4fe","title":"Fix snake_case registration classification in extension conformance matrix","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-14T03:06:25.776469293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:28:08.367334092Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T03:28:08.367312031Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3h8w","title":"Risk review: license + security + runtime dependencies","description":"# Goal
Filter or flag candidates with licensing, security, or runtime‑dependency risks.

# Deliverables
- License compatibility check (MIT/BSD/Apache preferred; note GPL/copyleft).
- Security red flags (abandoned repos, unsafe install scripts, opaque binaries).
- Runtime dependency risks (Node/Bun requirements vs Pi constraints).

# Notes
This review informs final inclusion/exclusion decisions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:48.290384961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:11:28.158655720Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:11:28.158567756Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h8w","depends_on_id":"bd-250p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3h8w","depends_on_id":"bd-38dx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3h8w","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2453,"issue_id":"bd-3h8w","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Some extensions are popular but risky (license conflicts, unsafe install scripts, heavy runtimes).

Reasoning: A risk filter avoids validating extensions we cannot safely ship or support.

Considerations: Document any risky items kept for coverage reasons and include mitigation notes.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:50:13Z"},{"id":2454,"issue_id":"bd-3h8w","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Risk review with evidence log.

Deliverables:
1. **License compatibility check**: 208 artifacts scanned. 179 (86%) clear permissive (178 MIT + 1 Apache-2.0). 29 (14%) UNKNOWN license. 0 GPL/copyleft. Detection via LICENSE files, package.json, and provenance manifest fallback.

2. **Security red flags**: 208 artifacts scanned with 13-pattern security scanner. 107 clean, 95 info-only (child_process, .env refs - common in extensions), 6 warnings (eval, new Function, localStorage - all legitimate extension patterns). 0 critical findings. No abandoned repos or opaque binaries in vendored artifacts.

3. **Runtime dependency risks**: 32 extensions have npm dependencies. 0 have heavy/native dependencies (puppeteer, node-pty, grpc, etc.). All dependencies are pure-JS/TS packages compatible with QuickJS runtime constraints.

Overall risk: MEDIUM (due to 6 security warnings and 29 unknown licenses).

Evidence log: tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/RISK_REVIEW.json (schema: pi.ext.risk_review.v1)
- Per-artifact: license, security severity, npm deps, risk level (minimal/low/medium/high)
- Sections: copyleft_extensions (empty), security_flagged (6), npm_dependency_risks (32)
- Test: tests/ext_risk_review.rs (asserts 0 critical security findings)","created_at":"2026-02-07T04:11:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ha23","title":"Delete SQLite WAL/SHM sidecars when session picker removes sqlite sessions","description":"Fresh-eyes review: src/session_picker.rs deletes the primary .sqlite session file but leaves SQLite WAL/SHM siblings behind. Because session_sqlite.rs enables journal_mode=WAL, picker deletion currently leaks recent session data and disk usage. Extend deletion to remove/trash *.sqlite-wal and *.sqlite-shm only after the primary delete succeeds, and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:22:24.021911477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:39.369429373Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:39.369406731Z","close_reason":"Implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
